definition: Used to introduce the contrasting or opposite perspective
example: Recursion is elegant. On the flip side, it risks stack overflow.

definition: Expressing the main facts about something concisely
example: In a nutshell, a linked list has O(1) insert but O(n) search.

word: at the end of the day
definition: When everything is taken into consideration
example: At the end of the day, readability beats micro-optimisation.

definition: A rough numerical estimate
example: As a ballpark figure, this handles about one million requests a day.

definition: To start an activity or process
example: Let me get the ball rolling by walking through the base case.

definition: To start something energetically and make immediate progress
example: With this scaffold, new developers can hit the ground running.

definition: To endure a painful or difficult situation that is unavoidable
example: We have to bite the bullet and refactor the entire auth module.

definition: To do something in the easiest or cheapest way, sacrificing quality
example: We cannot cut corners on input validation — security depends on it.

definition: To think creatively and beyond conventional approaches
example: To solve this efficiently we need to think outside the box.

definition: An obvious problem that no one wants to discuss
example: The elephant in the room is that our database has no indexing.

definition: To waste time creating something that already exists
example: We should use a library for this rather than reinvent the wheel.

definition: Fully informed or knowledgeable about something
example: I need a day to get up to speed on the new architecture.

definition: Having a shared understanding of a situation
example: Let's make sure we are on the same page regarding the requirements.

definition: To briefly make contact or reconnect with someone
example: Let's touch base tomorrow after the build finishes.

definition: Tasks or goals that are easily achievable
example: Caching the static assets is low-hanging fruit for performance.

definition: Strictly according to the rules
example: We handled the migration completely by the book to avoid data loss.

definition: A problem or situation that occurs only outside of normal operating parameters
example: This bug only appears in a very specific corner case.

definition: Source code that has a complex and tangled control structure
example: If we don't refactor now, this will turn into spaghetti code.

definition: A simple and seemingly magical solution to a complicated problem
example: There is no silver bullet for distributed consensus.

definition: To make a noticeable difference
example: Optimising this loop won't really move the needle on overall latency.

definition: To undertake an impossible task or make a project unnecessarily broad
example: Let's start with an MVP instead of trying to boil the ocean.

definition: A broadly accurate guide or principle
example: As a rule of thumb, avoid nested loops when dealing with large datasets.

definition: To stop something at an early stage before it becomes a problem
example: We caught the memory leak early and nipped it in the bud.

definition: To begin from the very beginning
example: The legacy system is beyond repair; we need to start from scratch.

definition: To start planning a completely new approach after a failure
example: The tests failed completely, so it's back to the drawing board.
